name: Ricote Valley and Quípar Reservoir Loop
name_original: Marcha Cicloturista de la Conserva — circular desde Molino de Segura
description: Cycle the challenging 69.6-mile March of the Preserve from Molino de Segura, featuring panoramic views and reservoir sights.
region_intro: Road cycling around Molina de Segura offers diverse terrain across agricultural plains, fertile valleys, and gentle mountains in the Murcia region of Spain. The area is characterized by the Segura River, which winds through green fields and Mediterranean fruit plantations, and features dramatic red limestone cliffs at Cortao de las Peñas. Nearby Sierra Espuña Regional Park provides additional challenging climbs and well-maintained roads. This varied landscape provides a range of road cycling experiences for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Molina De Segura The most popular road cycling route is Ricote Valley and Quípar Reservoir Loop, a 69.6 miles (112.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain as it traverses the scenic Ricote Valley and passes the Quípar Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Fortuna and Cortao de las Peñas Loop from Molina de Segura, a moderate 30.8 miles (49.5 km) path. This route explores the areas around Fortuna and the distinctive red limestone cliffs of Cortao de las Peñas. Local road cyclists also love the Murcia and Alcantarilla via the Northwest Greenway and the Segura River — circular from Molina de Segura, a 21.9 miles (35.3 km) trail leading through agricultural plains and along the Segura River, often completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes. Road cycling around Molina de Segura is defined by agricultural plains, the Segura River, and dramatic limestone cliffs. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Molina de Segura are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 2.0 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Molina de Segura's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Molina de Segura?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Molina de Segura, offering a wide range of experiences. These include 9 easy routes, 30 moderate routes, and 16 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Molina de Segura?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from agricultural plains and fertile valleys along the Segura River to gentle mountains. You'll encounter green fields, Mediterranean fruit plantations, and dramatic red limestone cliffs at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2760010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortao de las Peñas&lt;/a&gt;. For more challenging climbs, the nearby Sierra Espuña Regional Park provides well-maintained roads through mountain ranges.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Molina de Segura?
A: Molina de Segura benefits from a mild climate, making it suitable for road cycling year-round. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular loops. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008393804/ricote-valley-and-quipar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricote Valley and Quípar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008373194/fortuna-and-cortao-de-las-penas-loop-from-molina-de-segura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortuna and Cortao de las Peñas Loop from Molina de Segura&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while road cycling in Molina de Segura?
A: You can encounter several natural landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2760010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortao de las Peñas&lt;/a&gt;, with its dramatic red limestone cliffs, is a prominent feature. Routes also often follow the scenic Segura River and pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3152197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Contraparada of the Segura River&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Abanilla&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4988220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de La Pila&lt;/a&gt; are also notable natural points of interest.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the cycling routes?
A: While focusing on outdoor activities, Molina de Segura and its surroundings offer historical elements. You can explore the Museum of the Wall, visit the Castle viewpoint for panoramic views, or venture slightly further to see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2925673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murcia Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3833926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Casino of Murcia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Molina de Segura?
A: Yes, there are easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008712349/murcia-and-alcantarilla-via-the-northwest-greenway-and-the-segura-river-circular-from-molina-de-segura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murcia and Alcantarilla via the Northwest Greenway and the Segura River — circular from Molina de Segura&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 21.9-mile (35.3 km) trail that leads through agricultural plains and along the Segura River, offering a pleasant experience for all.

Q: Are there any dedicated cycling paths or greenways?
A: Yes, the broader Murcia region, accessible from Molina de Segura, features over 170 kilometers of 'Greenways' (Vías Verdes). These dedicated cycling and walking trails often follow old railway lines or river paths, providing safe and scenic routes away from traffic.

Q: What are the options for advanced road cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: Advanced cyclists can find challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008393804/ricote-valley-and-quipar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricote Valley and Quípar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at 69.6 miles (112.1 km) with over 1200m of ascent, is a difficult option. Additionally, the nearby Sierra Espuña Regional Park is renowned for its challenging climbs, such as the Collado Bermejo, and well-maintained mountain roads.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Molina de Segura?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 2.0 stars from more than 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from the scenic agricultural plains along the Segura River to the more challenging climbs and dramatic landscapes like the Cortao de las Peñas.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008393804/ricote-valley-and-quipar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricote Valley and Quípar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 69.6 miles (112.1 km). Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008636362/altos-de-caputa-collado-de-la-calera-ricote-ulea-altorreal-circular-from-molina-de-segura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altos de Caputa - Collado de la Calera - Ricote - Ulea - Altorreal — circular from Molina de Segura&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 100 km and features significant elevation.

Beautiful picture of the Segura river as it passes through Cieza from the Iron Bridge.
From the dam you can enjoy impressive views of the reservoir and the canyon. Definitely worth a stop.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Cycling March of the Preserve route?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a difficult racebike route, demanding very good fitness from cyclists. It covers 112 kilometers with an elevation gain of 1,280 meters, making it a significant undertaking.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain features a mix of well-maintained asphalt roads, typical of the Murcia region, but also includes segments that may be unpaved and challenging. Be prepared for exciting climbs to nearby mountain passes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is estimated to take around 5 hours and 18 minutes to complete for a racebike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of views and natural features can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect breathtaking panoramic views throughout the journey. The route traverses areas with green fields and Mediterranean fruit plantations. Notable points of interest include the Azud de Ojós Reservoir, views of the Segura River from Puente de Hierro, and the Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam and Quípar River Canyon.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this route in Molino de Segura?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, as the route is circular from Molino de Segura, you can typically find parking in or around the town center. Look for public parking lots or street parking that allows for longer durations.

faqs.question: Is the Cycling March of the Preserve route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebikes and covers long distances on roads, some of which may be unpaved. It is generally not suitable for cycling with dogs, especially given the challenging nature and potential for traffic.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Murcia region benefits from a favorable Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for cycling almost year-round. However, spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) offer the most pleasant temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It passes through ZEPA Sierra de Ricote y La Navela and ZEPA Sierra del Molino, Embalse del Quípar y Llanos del Cagitán, which are protected areas, but access for cycling is generally unrestricted on public roads and trails.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through areas with charming towns. While specific cafes are not listed, you can expect to find opportunities for refreshments and food in these towns, especially closer to Molino de Segura and other populated areas along the way.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially follows sections of several official trails, including Río Segura, Transmurciana 22, and Camino de Santiago del Azahar.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging racebike route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, it's essential to bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling gear, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Consider a GPS device for navigation, as some sections might be less intuitive.
